McCook Ben Nelson Regional Airport Wikipedia

note: Currently EAS-served by Denver Air Connection to DEN only

lostDestinations.source: Rapid City Journal - Chadron Airport History article; CAB route abandonment ruling 1961
Rapid City Journal - Air service in Chadron could change, 2023

note: Boutique Air operated CDR-RAP briefly Feb-May 2023 before losing EAS contract; Southern Airways Express took over DEN route June 2023

lostDestinations.source: North Platte Regional Airport Wikipedia - United Airlines/Boeing Transport 1929, Frontier until 1984
North Platte Regional Airport Wikipedia

note: Currently EAS-served by SkyWest/United Express to DEN

lostDestinations.source: Western Nebraska Regional Airport Wikipedia - Frontier ended service 1984 on main routes, Twin Otters on branch routes ~1980
Western Nebraska Regional Airport Wikipedia - Frontier left 1984

note: Western Airlines served 1945-1959, United 1953-1959, Frontier 1959-1984; currently EAS-served by SkyWest/United Express to DEN

lostDestinations.source: Central Nebraska Regional Airport Wikipedia - Great Lakes served DEN 1996-2003
Central Nebraska Regional Airport Wikipedia
Central Nebraska Regional Airport Wikipedia - Air Wisconsin 1979-1981, Rocky Mountain 1985-1987

note: Currently has Allegiant service (independent) and American Eagle EAS service to ORD

lostDestinations.source: Norfolk Regional Airport Wikipedia - EAS ended May 2004, too close to Omaha (<210 mi)
Norfolk Regional Airport Wikipedia

note: EAS subsidies ended 2004 per federal rule barring subsidy >$200/pax within 210 miles of medium/large hub; Omaha ~100 miles away

lostDestinations.source: Great Bend Municipal Airport Wikipedia - Great Lakes DEN service ended 2014
Great Bend Municipal Airport Wikipedia - SeaPort shutdown Jan 16 2016

note: Lost all scheduled service in 2016 when SeaPort Airlines shut down; last served MCI and ICT

lostDestinations.source: Hays Regional Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest served HYS 1969-2007 with Wichita flights
Hays Regional Airport Wikipedia - Great Lakes flew to MCI 2007-2009

note: Currently EAS-served by SkyWest/United Express to DEN; Great Lakes DEN service ended 2014

lostDestinations.source: Liberal Mid-America Regional Airport Wikipedia - Central Airlines 1956, Frontier left 1981
Liberal Mid-America Regional Airport Wikipedia - Frontier served regional route through DDC and LBL

note: Currently EAS-served by SkyWest/United Express to DEN; Frontier left 1981, large service gap before EAS reinstatement

lostDestinations.source: Dodge City Regional Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest served routes through Wichita
Dodge City Regional Airport Wikipedia

note: Currently EAS-served to DEN; Continental started 1939, Central/Frontier followed, Air Midwest served until ~2007

lostDestinations.source: Garden City Regional Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest served Wichita until 2007
Garden City Regional Airport Wikipedia - Great Lakes United Express DEN until 2012
Garden City Regional Airport Wikipedia - Great Lakes added MCI after Air Midwest left in 2007, ended 2012

note: All pre-2012 service replaced by American Eagle to DFW in 2012; Continental began 1939

lostDestinations.source: Salina Regional Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest served Wichita 1972-1976 and 1983-2001; Great Lakes briefly 2000-2001
Salina Regional Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest/TWA Express served STL in 1990s

note: SeaPort served 2014-2016; Great Lakes returned 2016-2018 to DEN; currently served by Denver Air Connection EAS to DEN; Frontier served with 737s to DEN and ORD 1978-1983

lostDestinations.source: Hastings Municipal Airport Wikipedia - Frontier last Convair 580 left 1979
Hastings Municipal Airport Wikipedia

detail: Frontier Airlines Convair 580 turboprops served Hastings from 1959 until 1979; Mid-West Airlines had initiated service in 1950 on the city's first scheduled flights.

lostDestinations.source: Columbus Municipal Airport Wikipedia - Frontier Convair 580 service, last flights 1979
Columbus Municipal Airport Wikipedia

detail: Frontier Airlines operated Convair 580 turboprops to Columbus from the mid-1960s until 1979; service originated with Mid-West Airlines DC-3s in 1950.

lostDestinations.source: Sidney Municipal Airport Wikipedia - Frontier DC-3s from 1959, last Twin Otter 1980
Sidney Municipal Airport Wikipedia - Frontier served western Nebraska route through Cheyenne

detail: Frontier Airlines provided Douglas DC-3 service beginning 1959, upgraded to Twin Otter, with final departure in 1980; no scheduled service since.

lostDestinations.source: Topeka Regional Airport Wikipedia - United Express to ORD ended September 2, 2014
Topeka Regional Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest ended Topeka service November 2003
Topeka Regional Airport Wikipedia
Topeka Regional Airport Wikipedia - Frontier 737 service ended when Frontier collapsed 1986

detail: Topeka Regional Airport (Forbes Field) had peak service of 180,000 passengers/year in the 1980s with United, TWA, and Midway; the last airline, SkyWest/United Express, ended ORD service September 2, 2014.
source: Topeka Regional Airport Wikipedia; mtaa-topeka.org history page

lostDestinations.source: Hutchinson Municipal Airport Wikipedia - service to Denver ended ~1990
Hutchinson Municipal Airport Wikipedia - direct flights to Kansas City ended ~1990
Hutchinson Municipal Airport Wikipedia - Wichita service ended ~1990

detail: Commercial airlines served Hutchinson from 1944 to 1990 with direct flights to Denver, Kansas City, and Wichita; proximity (~50 miles) to Wichita's larger airport has prevented any airline resumption.

lostDestinations.source: Tri-City Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest served MCI 1977-1989
Tri-City Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest served Tulsa and Wichita from Parsons
Tri-City Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest served Parsons-Wichita 1977-1989

detail: Air Midwest operated 17-seat Swearingen Metroliner service from 1977 to April 1989, briefly codesharing as Eastern Express (1986-1988) and Braniff Express (1988-1989); no scheduled service since.

lostDestinations.source: Goodland Municipal Airport Wikipedia - Great Lakes DEN service ended early 2000
Goodland Municipal Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest served MCI until Mesa took over DEN route 1992

detail: Central Airlines served Goodland from 1961; Frontier followed 1967-1977; Air Midwest through 1992; Mesa/Great Lakes as United Express through 2000 when Great Lakes ended service and Goodland lost all scheduled flights.

lostDestinations.source: Coffeyville Municipal Airport Wikipedia - Ozark DC-3 Tulsa-KC with Coffeyville stop 1951-1954
Coffeyville Municipal Airport Wikipedia

detail: Ozark Airlines operated Douglas DC-3 service between Tulsa and Kansas City with a Coffeyville stop from 1951 to 1954; no scheduled service since.

lostDestinations.source: Manhattan Regional Airport Wikipedia - Frontier 737 service to Wichita ended 1986 when Frontier collapsed

lostDestinations.carrier: Air Midwest/US Airways Express (then Great Lakes)
lostDestinations.source: Manhattan Regional Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest/Mesa served MCI; Great Lakes ended April 7 2010
Manhattan Regional Airport Wikipedia - Great Lakes took over March 30 2008, ended April 7 2010 after American Eagle announced ORD/DFW expansion

detail: Manhattan lost EAS-dependent Kansas City and Denver service when Great Lakes Airlines departed April 7, 2010, replaced by American Eagle regional jets to ORD and DFW; airport is now diminished from its broader multi-hub network.

lostDestinations.source: Great Bend Municipal Airport Wikipedia - Great Lakes DEN service ended 2014
Great Bend Municipal Airport Wikipedia - SeaPort served MCI until shutdown Jan 16 2016
Great Bend Municipal Airport Wikipedia - SeaPort served Wichita until shutdown Jan 16 2016

detail: Great Bend lost all scheduled service on January 16, 2016 when SeaPort Airlines shut down entirely; the airport had continuous service from the late 1940s through Frontier, Air Midwest, Mesa, Great Lakes, and finally SeaPort.

lostDestinations.source: Salina Regional Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest Wichita service ended ~2001
Salina Regional Airport Wikipedia - TWA/Air Midwest STL service ended ~2003
Salina Regional Airport Wikipedia - Air Midwest US Airways Express MCI service ended 2007

note: Currently EAS-served to DEN; Great Lakes served 2016-2018 before closing; SeaPort 2014-2016; Frontier served with 737s 1978-1983 including ORD and DEN
